name: Generate contracts with HTTP / REST API and MySQL and log the results

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03349-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Download Source File

### Download Source File

GET ={{ $json.fileUrl }} — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Parse CSV Contents · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Parse CSV Contents

Parses the incoming CSV binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming document records to a stable schema

Next: Rows Pass Validation?

### Rows Pass Validation?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.documentId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Load Rows into MySQL · ②→ Quarantine Bad Rows

### Quarantine Bad Rows

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Load Rows into MySQL

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Align Field Names · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Sort Documents Newest First

### Sort Documents Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Send Summary to Telegram

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Generate contracts with HTTP / REST API and MySQL and log the results

**Pattern:** Document Generation · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Generate contracts with HTTP / REST API and MySQL and log the results. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual ocr & extraction is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Download Source File → Parse CSV Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Rows Pass Validation? → Load Rows into MySQL → Align Field Names → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to document generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating ocr & extraction in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; MySQL connection; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Bad rows are quarantined into their own store, not dropped silently and not allowed to break the whole batch — one malformed row in a 10,000-row file shouldn't stop the other 9,999 from loading, but it also shouldn't vanish without a trace.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Feed it a file with a few deliberately malformed rows and confirm they land in quarantine, not the main store.
2. Switch the source format (e.g. csv to xlsx) and see how little actually has to change.
3. Turn on archive mode and confirm the processed file gets repacked and stored.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your ocr & extraction process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
